title: hakea sessilis
intro: Hakea sessilis, also known as the Drooping Hakea, is a species of tree or shrub in the family Proteaceae, native to Western Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine to treat various ailments, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Hakea sessilis has been reported to have potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. The plant is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and its leaves and stems can also be used in a poultice or applied directly to the skin in traditional herbal preparations.
